spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates `::scroll-marker { content: counter(chapter) / 'Chapter ' counter(chapter) }` — the canonical motivating pattern from the spec discussion.

interactive.rationale: Toggle between old/new with a simulated a11y readout panel.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises Tab-vs-Chapter comparison; the demo delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens with a non-counter alt string, or how this interacts with the CSS scroll-marker-group property.

openQuestions.title: Test with a real screen reader
openQuestions.detail: The 'simulated a11y readout' is a div labelled like a screen reader output — but real screen readers may speak the alt-text differently. Document the test matrix (NVDA / VoiceOver / TalkBack) and reference test results.

openQuestions.title: Show interaction with scroll-marker-group
openQuestions.detail: The sibling feature in v140 is `css-scroll-target-group-property`. Demonstrate the labels working inside a real scroll-marker-group container so visitors see the full pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover counters() multi-level case
openQuestions.detail: Pair with the nested-toc sibling concept. Demonstrate that the spec change applies to counters() as well as counter() — show 'Chapter 1.2' alt vs '1.2'.

summary: The most direct motivation demo in the set — Tab 8 vs Chapter 8 is exactly what the spec discussion uses. Real-screen-reader verification would close the credibility gap.
